zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon於大約1615年出生在英國。她於1638年2月26日與John Fowles Jr在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1689年在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon于大约1615年出生在英国。她于1638年2月26日与John Fowles Jr在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1689年在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Lyon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Lyons
meanings: Irish: adopted for Ó Laighin (see Lyne , Lane , Lyon ) or Ó Liatháin (see Lehane ).

variants.name: Lyall
meanings: Scottish: relationship name from a pet form Lyon or of its diminutive Lionel. Compare Lyon .

variants.name: Lion
meanings: English and French: variant of   Lyon   1–3.
French: habitational name from Lion, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Jewish: associative variant of Levy , for instance chosen by several families in the Netherlands c. 1811.

variants.name: Louden
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Loudoun near Cunningham in Ayrshire, named in Celtic as Lugūnon ‘fort of Lugus’ (see Lyon 2).
Scottish: possibly a variant of   Lothian  .

variants.name: Leon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon was born about 1615, in England. She married John Fowles Jr on 26 February 1638, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1689, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon est née environ 1615, à Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Fowles Jr le 26 février 1638, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1689, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon wurde um 1615 in England geboren. Sie hat am 26. Februar 1638 in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom John Fowles Jr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1689 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon è nata il circa 1615 nel England. Lei sposato John Fowles Jr il 26 febbraio 1638, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1689, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolta a Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyonは約1615年にイギリスで生まれた。1638年2月26日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでJohn Fowles Jrと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1689年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon(은)는 대략 1615년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1638년 2월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 John Fowles Jr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1689년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon nasceu aproximadamente 1615, em Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Fowles Jr em 26 de fevereiro de 1638, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1689, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon родилась около 1615 г. в местности Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Fowles Jr 26 февраля 1638 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1689 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Lyon nació aproximadamente 1615, en Inglaterra. Se casó con John Fowles Jr el 26 de febrero de 1638, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1689, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
